mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS path lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive when the policy rate is trending down from an already restrictive level.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline after FEDFUNDS has declined for at least two consecutive monthly observations, or if the sign of the relation is negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, the market treats policy as restrictive; any subsequent decline in the policy rate boosts rate-sensitive valuation and lifts 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate falls below 55% over any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS signals weakening household income and deteriorating earnings expectations, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal does not outperform either UNRATE or PAYEMS alone, or if episodes with rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS still generate positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue growth expectations, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not precede above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the conditional mean return is not positive after controlling for the sign of the 3-month change.

mechanism_narrative: Strength in INDPRO signals improving real activity and capacity utilization; when industrial output is accelerating, equity earnings revisions follow and S&P 500 returns tend to be positive over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in GDPC1 and HOUST marks broad demand expansion in output and housing, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through stronger revenue growth expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined GDPC1-HOUST signal is not stronger than each series alone, or if joint-expansion periods do not deliver positive 60-day SPX returns at least 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is falling while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real-rate pressure is easing and the equity discount-rate channel improves, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change < 0 and FEDFUNDS > CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation plus elevated policy rates does not produce positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips negative during more than one third of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle labor cooling regime, a rising UNRATE paired with declining PAYEMS reflects weakening payroll demand and higher recession odds, which should compress equity prices and generate negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-slowing signal fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if triggered periods produce positive returns more often than negative returns over any 4-quarter rolling window.
